UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile within the CRM application, storing essential information beyond authentication credentials for display, assignment, and team management purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, typically corresponding to the user's authentication ID (e.g., Firebase Auth UID).

email.description: The primary email address of the user.

displayName.description: The public display name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the CRM system, defining access levels and responsibilities.

photoUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Lead.title: Lead
Lead.description: Represents a potential customer or sales opportunity, tracking their journey through the sales pipeline.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lead entity.

ownerUid.description: Reference to UserProfile.id, indicating the user who owns or is assigned this lead. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Lead)

name.description: The full name of the lead contact.

email.description: The primary email address of the lead.

phone.description: The primary phone number of the lead.

company.description: The company associated with the lead.

status.description: The current stage of the lead in the sales pipeline.

dealValue.description: The estimated monetary value of the potential deal with this lead.

source.description: The origin or channel through which the lead was acquired (e.g., 'Website', 'Referral', 'Cold Call').

tags.description: An array of descriptive tags or keywords associated with the lead.

leadScore.description: A numerical score indicating the quality or priority of the lead.

nextFollowUpAt.description: Timestamp for the next scheduled follow-up or contact with the lead.

lastContactAt.description: Timestamp of the most recent interaction or contact with the lead.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the lead record was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the lead record was last updated.

customFields.description: A flexible object for storing additional, user-defined fields specific to the lead. (Represented as an array of generic objects to allow for flexibility with varying data types and structures for custom fields)

notesCount.description: Denormalized count of notes associated with this lead.

callsCount.description: Denormalized count of calls logged for this lead.

Activity.title: Activity
Activity.description: Records an interaction or significant event related to a specific lead, contributing to the lead's history and timeline.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Activity entity.

leadId.description: Reference to Lead.id, indicating which lead this activity pertains to. (Relationship: Lead 1:N Activity)

ownerUid.description: Reference to UserProfile.id, indicating the user who logged this activity. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Activity)

content.description: The detailed description or content of the activity (e.g., call notes, meeting summary, status change reason).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the activity was recorded.

oldStatus.description: The previous status of the lead, applicable if the activity type is 'status_change'.

newStatus.description: The new status of the lead, applicable if the activity type is 'status_change'.

tag.description: The specific tag that was added, applicable if the activity type is 'tag_added'.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents a scheduled event, meeting, or reminder, often associated with a lead and visible on the calendar.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

ownerUid.description: Reference to UserProfile.id, indicating the user who created or owns this event. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Event)

leadId.description: Reference to Lead.id, indicating which lead this event is associated with (optional, as some events may not be lead-specific). (Relationship: Lead 0..1:N Event)

title.description: A concise title or subject for the event.

description.description: A detailed description or agenda for the event.

startAt.description: The starting date and time of the event.

endAt.description: The ending date and time of the event (optional for single-point reminders or tasks).

allDay.description: A boolean indicating if the event is an all-day event.

eventType.description: The category or nature of the event (e.g., 'follow-up', 'meeting', 'task', 'reminder').

status.description: The current status of the event (e.g., 'scheduled', 'completed', 'cancelled').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the event record was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the event record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores user profile details, where the document ID is the user's Firebase Auth UID. Access is read/write for the profile owner and typically read-only for other authenticated users for purposes like lead assignment. Includes denormalized 'role' field, but critical roles use a separate collection.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier (Firebase Auth UID) of the user whose profile is being stored.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for defining administrative roles. The document ID corresponds to the Firebase Auth UID of an administrator. Authorization for admin actions is granted by the existence of a document with the user's UID in this collection, simplifying DBAC rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier (Firebase Auth UID) of a user designated as an administrator.

definition.description: Stores individual lead records. Includes the 'ownerUid' field which links to the UserProfile.id, enabling authorization independence and efficient querying for leads assigned to a specific user. Admins can access all leads.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific lead.

definition.description: Records interactions and events related to a specific lead. Each document includes 'leadId', 'ownerUid' (the user who logged the activity), and 'leadOwnerUid' (denormalized from the parent Lead) to ensure authorization independence. Access is granted to the lead's owner or the activity creator.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent lead for this activity.

params.name: activityId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific activity.

definition.description: Stores personal calendar events and follow-ups for a specific user. The 'ownerUid' field in the document must match the '{userId}' in the path, ensuring strict user-based ownership and authorization independence for personal events. Includes an optional 'leadId' for lead-related context.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier (Firebase Auth UID) of the user who owns this event.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific event.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering strictly to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (Strategy A):**
- For `Lead` documents in `/leads/{leadId}`, the `ownerUid` field is directly stored within each document. This ensures that authorization for a lead (who can read/write it) can be determined solely by checking `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerUid`, eliminating the need for `get()` operations on parent documents or separate user roles.
- For `Activity` documents within `/leads/{leadId}/activities/{activityId}`, the `leadId`, the `ownerUid` (of the activity creator), and critically, the `leadOwnerUid` (denormalized from the parent Lead) are stored within each activity document. This allows security rules for activities to be evaluated atomically by comparing `request.auth.uid` against these fields, making them independent of the parent lead's document read.
- For `Event` documents in `/users/{userId}/events/{eventId}`, the `ownerUid` field is present and is expected to match the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This intrinsically links the event to its owner, enabling atomic authorization checks without fetching the parent user profile.

**Structural Segregation & QAPs (Strategy B & C.1, C.2, C.3, C.4):**
- **`/userProfiles/{userId}`:** This collection stores user-specific profiles. It follows the "Private Data" and "Hierarchical Paths for User-Owned Data" pattern for read/write access by the owner, and controlled list access for other users (e.g., to assign leads). This provides a homogeneous security posture for individual profile management.
- **`/roles_admin/{userId}`:** This collection is a prime example of "DBAC (Existence over Content)" for global roles. The mere existence of a document with a user's UID in this collection grants administrative privileges, allowing for straightforward `exists()` checks in security rules to enable QAPs for viewing sensitive or global data (e.g., all leads).
- **`/leads/{leadId}`:** This top-level collection centralizes all lead data, accommodating the application's requirement for a "Leads Page" and "Pipeline / Kanban View" that can display leads across all users (for admins) or specific users (for sales reps). By including `ownerUid` in each document, it enables Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) such as `db.collection('leads').where('ownerUid', '==', request.auth.uid)` for sales reps to retrieve only their assigned leads. Admins, with appropriate `exists()` rule checks, can query the entire collection.
- **`/leads/{leadId}/activities/{activityId}`:** Activities are tightly coupled to leads, so a subcollection ensures semantic grouping. The denormalization of `leadOwnerUid` means that a user can list activities for a lead if they are the `leadOwnerUid`, which is a QAP.
- **`/users/{userId}/events/{eventId}`:** Events are personal calendar items. Placing them under a user's path (`/users/{userId}/...`) ensures that each user has a private calendar. QAPs for the calendar page (e.g., fetching all events for `request.auth.uid`) are directly supported and highly efficient, as rules can simply enforce `request.auth.uid == userId` for all read/write operations.

This architecture ensures that security rules are simple, explicit, and do not rely on expensive or non-atomic `get()` calls, making the system inherently more secure, performant, and easier to debug.
